instructions:
Normalize the Troubleshooting section across the four quickstart pages. Current state: accordion naming is inconsistent ('Build Issues' on iOS/Android, 'Installation Issues' on Flutter/Web; 'Platform Issues' on Flutter only).

Target structure — every page MUST have these three accordions in this order, with these exact titles and icons:

1. `Installation Issues` (icon: `wrench`) — package/dependency/build-tool problems specific to getting the SDK installed and compiling. iOS framework-not-found, Android dependency conflicts, Flutter package-not-found, Web module-not-found all go here.
2. `Runtime Issues` (icon: `bug`) — SDK-not-initialized, auth failures, network/permission errors.
3. `Platform-Specific Issues` (icon: `mobile` for mobile platforms, `globe` for web) — anything that's genuinely platform-quirk (iOS M1/Rosetta, Android RxJava, Flutter native build, Web browser compat).

Rules:
- Preserve all existing content. Move bullet points between accordions if they're currently in the wrong one (e.g. iOS 'M1 Mac Simulator' is currently under 'Build Issues' but belongs under 'Platform-Specific Issues').
- Do NOT delete content. If an accordion would be empty after re-sorting, leave it with a single 'No known issues.' line so the structure stays consistent.
- Do NOT add new troubleshooting content. This is a reorganization-only task.
- Preserve the surrounding `<AccordionGroup>` wrapper and any non-troubleshooting content on the page.

Report in the result's `notes` field: per file, the list of bullets moved between accordions.

validators.name: all_four_have_three_canonical_accordions
validators.command:
python3 -c "import re, sys; failed=[]; canonical=['Installation Issues','Runtime Issues','Platform-Specific Issues'];
for f in ['social-plus-sdk/getting-started/platform-setup/mobile/ios-quick-start.mdx','social-plus-sdk/getting-started/platform-setup/mobile/android-quick-start.mdx','social-plus-sdk/getting-started/platform-setup/mobile/flutter-quick-start.mdx','social-plus-sdk/getting-started/platform-setup/web/web-quick-start.mdx']:
  t=open(f).read()
  # find Troubleshooting section
  m=re.search(r'## Troubleshooting(.*?)(?=\n## |\Z)', t, re.DOTALL)
  if not m: failed.append(f'{f}: no Troubleshooting section'); continue
  body=m.group(1)
  titles=re.findall(r'<Accordion title="([^"]+)"', body)
  for c in canonical:
    if c not in titles: failed.append(f'{f}: missing accordion "{c}" (has: {titles})')
print('\n'.join(failed) if failed else 'OK'); sys.exit(1 if failed else 0)"
